title: 8 Branch Capture User Management
body_text:
8	Branch Capture User Management
8.1	Overview of Branch Capture Access and Administration
Users access the Branch Capture application via the Client Centre. Each user must be set up as either a Client Centre User or a User Management Security Officer and assigned access to Branch Capture. User Management Security Officers must have administrative access to Branch Capture to create Branch Capture Users.
Use Section 8.2, Branch Capture Workflows and User Types, to determine the user types to assign to Branch Capture Users.
8.2	Branch Capture Workflows and User Types
There are four Branch Capture user types: Capture Users, Reviewers, Supervisors, and Administrators. Based on how you assign user types, a userГўВҖВҷs tasks, experience, and responsibilities vary. You decide which workflow or combination of workflows to assign to your Branch Capture Users. For example, you can have some branch staff that perform all the deposit processing tasks and others performing separate tasks (e.g., one user who creates deposits, and another user who reviews and transmits deposits).
When creating Branch Capture users, you should consider how you want to assign deposit processing duties. Internal policies may influence user set-up decisions. You can combine different user types depending on your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs location(s), needs, and branch size. Whatever workflow(s) you use, always ensure that you review deposits carefully and correct deposit errors before transmitting them to Central 1.
Administrator User Type
An Administrator user type is typically assigned to individuals in your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs head office who donГўВҖВҷt participate in the deposit process.
Important
:	DonГўВҖВҷt combine the Administrator user type with the other user types.
User Types
Assigned to
User Responsibilities
CU Administration Manager
(Administrator User)
Individuals at your head office.
Generates deposit reports for all locations of your financial institution.
Performs deposit research for your financial institution.
Note:	See Chapter 13, Branch Capture Reports and Research, for more information.
Branch Capture Deposit User Types and Workflows
This subsection describes Branch Capture deposit user types and their associated workflows, responsibilities, benefits, and considerations. The two recommended options are described in greater detail. Use these descriptions to evaluate your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs needs and determine how best to assign Branch Capture user types. See the User Management Part for instructions on setting up Branch Capture Users.
Option 1: Capture User Separate and Reviewer and Supervisor Users Combined
This workflow allows for the separation of deposit processing tasks. Capture Users create deposits, correct deposit errors, balance deposits, and submit deposits for review. Reviewer/Supervisor Users review deposits and either approve and transmit them to Central 1 for processing or disapprove them for correction by the Capture User. See Figure 1 for a diagram of this workflow.
Benefits
ГўВҖВ“ More experienced users monitor Capture UserГўВҖВҷs deposits to ensure they are error-free and balanced before transmitting them to Central 1 for processing. If an error is detected, the deposit can be disapproved for a Capture User to correct.
Considerations
ГўВҖВ“ Reviewer/Supervisor Users must monitor the Branch Capture deposit list queues for deposits regularly throughout the day. Ensure that users understand their roles and responsibilities. Deposits containing errors are subject to deposit adjustments. See Section 17.3 Deposit Adjustments.
Note:	Deposit adjustments are subject to fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.
Permission(s) Assigned
User Responsibilities
CU Branch Deposit Clerk ГўВҖВ“ Capture
(Capture User)
Creates deposits and scans deposit items
Ensures deposits are error-free and balanced
Completes deposits for review by a Reviewer/Supervisor User
Creates reports for owned deposits
The Capture User selects
Complete
when the deposit is error-free and balanced. The deposit can be edited and corrected if disapproved. The deposit isnГўВҖВҷt submitted for processing until itГўВҖВҷs reviewed and transmitted by a Reviewer/Supervisor User.
CU Branch Deposit Clerk ГўВҖВ“ Review and CU Branch Supervisor
(Reviewer/Supervisor User)
Reviews deposits for errors
Disapproves deposits with errors for Capture Users to correct
Reassigns deposits that havenГўВҖВҷt been completed (if required)
Approves and transmits deposits to Central 1 for processing
Generates deposit reports for assigned locations
Manages Aged Open deposits
Figure 1: Separate Capture and Reviewer and Supervisor Users Combined
Option 2:
Capture, Reviewer, and Supervisor User Combined
This workflow allows for all deposit tasks to be combined into one role and streamlines your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs deposit process. See Figure 2 for a diagram of this workflow.
Benefits
ГўВҖВ“ 	Individual users perform all deposit processing tasks. Deposits are transmitted when completed. Users can also review, disapprove/approve, and transmit deposit for Capture Users at assigned locations (if applicable).
Considerations
ГўВҖВ“ Capture/Reviewer/Supervisor Users are responsible for all deposit tasks (e.g., responsibility for ensuring deposits are error-free and balanced is with one individual). We recommend that you assign this workflow to more experienced individuals. Ensure that users understand their roles and responsibilities. Deposits containing errors are subject to deposit adjustments. See Section 17.3, Deposit Adjustments.
Note:	Deposit adjustments are subject to fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.
User Types Assigned
User Responsibilities
CU Branch Deposit Clerk ГўВҖВ“ Capture,
CU Branch Deposit Clerk ГўВҖВ“ Review, and
CU Branch Supervisor
(Capture + Reviewer + Supervisor User)
Creates deposits and scans deposit items
Ensures deposits are error-free and balanced
Completes deposit (automatically transmits)
Reviews deposits for Capture Users (if applicable)
Re-assigns deposits that havenГўВҖВҷt been completed (if applicable)
Manages Aged Open deposits
Disapproves deposits with errors for Capture Users (if applicable).
Generates deposit reports for assigned locations
The Capture and Supervisor User selects
Complete
when the deposit is error-free and balanced. The deposit automatically transmits to Central 1. ThereГўВҖВҷs no secondary review.
The deposit canГўВҖВҷt be edited after itГўВҖВҷs completed.
Figure 2: Combined Capture, Reviewer and Supervisor
Option 3: Capture, Reviewer, and Supervisor as Separate User Types (Not Recommended)
Benefits
ГўВҖВ“ Segregation of workflow and delineation of tasks.
Considerations
ГўВҖВ“ More staff involved in Branch Capture processing. Once the Reviewer approves a deposit, it canГўВҖВҷt be edited or deleted even if the Supervisor User finds an error. Ensure that users understand their roles and responsibilities. Deposits containing errors are subject to deposit adjustments. See Section 17.3, Deposit Adjustments.
Note:	Deposit adjustments are subject to fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.
User Types Assigned
User Responsibilities
CU Branch Deposit Clerk ГўВҖВ“ Capture
(Capture User)
Creates deposits and scans deposit items
Ensures deposits are error-free and balanced
Completes deposits for review by a Reviewer User
Creates reports for owned deposits
The Capture User selects
Complete
when the deposit is error-free and balanced. The deposit can be edited. The Deposit isnГўВҖВҷt submitted for processing until itГўВҖВҷs reviewed by a Reviewer User and transmitted by a Supervisor User.
CU Branch Deposit Clerk ГўВҖВ“ Review
(Reviewer User)
Reviews deposits for errors
Disapproves deposits with errors for Capture Users to correct or approves deposits for Supervisors to transmit to Central 1 for processing
Note: Once the Reviewer User approves a deposit, it canГўВҖВҷt be edited or deleted and must be transmitted to Central 1.
CU Branch Supervisor (Supervisor User)
Transmits deposits to Central 1 for processing
Generates deposit reports for assigned locations
Manages Aged Open deposits
8.3	Granting Branch Capture Access to a Client Centre User
See the User Management Part for information on granting Branch Capture Access to a Client Centre User.
8.4	Granting Branch Capture Access to an Existing User Management Security Officer
There are two ways to manage your User Management Security Officers, depending on the change required. You can either send a request to Central 1 to make a change on your behalf, or you can make the change in the User Management application.
If your financial institution has at least two existing User Management Security Officers with administrative access to Branch Capture, they can assign other User Management Security Officers with personal and/or administrative access to Branch Capture in the User Management application. See the User Management Part for more information. Otherwise, complete the User Management Security Officer Modification/Deletion Request, Form 2035, as described below.
To grant Branch Capture access to an existing User Management Security Officer via Central 1:
Complete the CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request, Form 2035. See Figure 7. Completion of the form is self-explanatory, but note the following:
ГўВ‘
Select
Add
.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Change Request Details ГўВҖВ“ Personal Access Rights
ГўВҲВ’
Record ГўВҖВңBranch CaptureГўВҖВқ to give the individual personal access to Branch Capture.
ГўВҲВ’
List the Branch Capture user types to which the individual requires access (e.g., Capture User, Reviewer, Supervisor, or Administrator). Administrators shouldnГўВҖВҷt be granted access to the other Branch Capture user types.
ГўВҲВ’
List the transit number(s) to which the individual requires access. For Administrators, record your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs head office transit number.
ГўВ‘Вў
Change Request Details ГўВҖВ“ Administrative Rights
ГўВҲВ’
Record ГўВҖВңBranch CaptureГўВҖВқ if the individual is authorized to assign Branch Capture application access to Client Centre Users and other User Management Security Officers (personal access only).
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Financial Institution Authorization
ГўВҲВ’
Ensure that two authorized signing officers sign the form.
Figure 7: CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request, Form 2035
Forward the form as directed on page 2. If applicable, the central credit union will authorize the form and forward it to Central 1.
Retain a copy of the form for your records.
Related Information
User Management Security Officer Modification/Deletion Request,
Form 2035
8.5	Granting Branch Capture Access to a New User Management Security Officer
User Management Security Officers canГўВҖВҷt create other User Management Security Officers. These requests must be sent to Central 1. To grant Branch Capture access to a new User Management Security Officer:
Complete the CPS Admin Appointment Certificate, Form 1918. See Figure 8. Completion of the form is self-explanatory, but note the following:
ГўВ‘
Personal Access Rights
ГўВҲВ’
Record ГўВҖВңBranch CaptureГўВҖВқ to give the individual personal access to Branch Capture.
ГўВҲВ’
List the Branch Capture user types to which the individual requires access (e.g., Capture User, Reviewer, Supervisor, or Administrator). Administrators shouldnГўВҖВҷt be granted access to the other Branch Capture user types.
ГўВҲВ’
List the transit number(s) to which the individual requires access. For Administrators, record your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs head office transit number.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Administrative Rights
ГўВҲВ’
Record ГўВҖВңBranch CaptureГўВҖВқ
if the individual is authorized to assign Branch Capture application access to Client Centre Users and other User Management Security Officers (personal access only).
ГўВ‘Вў
Financial Institution Authorization
ГўВҲВ’
Ensure that two signing officers sign the form.
Figure 8: CPS Admin Appointment Certificate, Form 1918
Forward the form as directed on page 2. If applicable, the central credit union will authorize the form and forward it to Central 1.
Retain a copy of the form for your records.
Central 1 will communicate a unique one-time password to new the User Management Security Officer. See the User Management Part for procedures on registering a Client Centre password.
Have another User Management Security Officer assign a 2-step security token to the new User Management Security Officer. See the 2-Step Security Part for procedures.
Related Information
User Management Security Officer Appointment Certificate,
Form 1918
END
